items.title: Fix Bedrock request handling to avoid 400 errors in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where AWS Bedrock was rejecting requests containing a top-level thinking field, causing 400 errors for approximately 5% of /v1/messages requests. The solution involves modifying the MessagesBaseHandler to detect Bedrock and strip the problematic thinking field from the request body, ensuring smooth operation for non-Bedrock upstreams. Key files modified include base.py, messages.py, and google.py.

items.title: Streamline Publisher with Playwright Alternatives in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update removes outdated and broken publishers, specifically `juejin.py` and `medium.py`, which have been replaced by more reliable Playwright-based versions. The new publishers, `juejin_browser.py` and `medium_playwright.py`, have been thoroughly tested and verified to ensure seamless publishing. Additionally, related tests have been updated or removed to reflect these changes.

items.title: Enhance vocal gender accuracy in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where the Suno API generated style tags with gender-inconsistent vocal references. A new function, fixVocalGenderInStyle, has been implemented to ensure that the style strings reflect the requested vocal gender. This change improves the accuracy of audio generation outputs, enhancing user experience.

items.title: Exciting New Browser Publishers for Medium and Juejin in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Playwright-based browser publishers for Medium and Juejin, replacing the previous API-based methods. The new implementations allow for direct interaction with the platforms' editors, enhancing reliability and bypassing anti-spam measures. Key files modified include the addition of browser automation scripts for both platforms, ensuring a smoother publishing experience.

items.title: Refactor SSO Token Endpoint for Clarity in Dify 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the internal SSO token URL from oauth2/v1/token to sso/v1/token, clarifying its purpose as an internal SSO code exchange rather than OAuth 2.0. The changes enhance the accuracy of the AceDataCloudOAuth helper, ensuring better alignment with its functionality. Overall, this refactor improves code readability and maintainability.

items.title: Refactor OAuth to SSO Operator and Update Endpoint in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the OAuthOperator to SSOOperator and modifies the related endpoint from /oauth2/v1 to /sso/v1. Key files affected include App.vue, auth.ts, and actions.ts, ensuring a consistent naming convention across the application. These changes enhance clarity and maintainability of the authentication process.

items.title: Refactor OAuth to SSO Operator and Update Endpoint in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the OAuthOperator to SSOOperator and modifies the related endpoint from /oauth2/v1 to /sso/v1. Changes were made across multiple files including nginx configuration, TypeScript operators, and the store index. These adjustments enhance clarity and align the codebase with the new SSO implementation.

items.title: Add /sso/ nginx proxy for updated SSO endpoint in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new nginx proxy configuration for the /sso/ path, aligning with the backend renaming of the OAuth2 token endpoint to /sso/v1/token. The change ensures seamless routing of SSO requests through the frontend, improving integration with the updated authentication backend.

items.title: Streamline CI to Prevent Version Collisions in V S Code M C P 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the marketplace-based daily build lookup from the publish workflow, generating extension versions using GitHub run metadata instead. This change prevents failures due to concurrent publish jobs attempting to use the same version number, ensuring smoother deployments. The new versioning formula has been validated to produce valid formats without conflicts.

items.title: Clean up README badges in M C P Veo 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the duplicated PyPI badge block from the README file, streamlining the badge section. The remaining badges have been kept unchanged to maintain clarity and functionality. This change enhances the overall presentation of the documentation without affecting any code.

items.title: Streamline README Badges in M C P Sora 🚀

items.title: Refactor SSO Token Endpoint for Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the internal SSO code-to-token exchange endpoint from 'oauth2/v1/token' to 'sso/v1/token', enhancing clarity and organization. The old path remains available as a deprecated alias to ensure a smooth transition for existing integrations. Key changes include updates to the views and URL configurations, impacting related components across multiple platforms.

These changes aim to streamline the deployment process and ensure better integration with external services.

items.summary: This update modifies the JetBrains version format to align with the PyPI standard (YYYY.M.D.BUILD) and updates the profile update token to use VSCODE_MCP_DISPATCH_TOKEN. These changes enhance version consistency and improve the workflow for profile updates.

items.title: CI/CD Fixes for JetBrains and Profile Update in M C P Luma 🚀
items.summary: This update includes two important CI/CD fixes. The JetBrains Plugin version format has been updated to align with PyPI's CalVer, preventing version conflicts when publishing. Additionally, the profile update token has been corrected to use the configured token, resolving the previous error related to missing authentication.

items.title: CI/CD Fixes for JetBrains and Profile Update in M C P Nano Banana 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two key CI/CD fixes. The JetBrains Plugin version format has been updated to `YYYY.M.D.BUILD` to align with PyPI CalVer, resolving version conflicts. Additionally, the Profile Update token has been corrected to use the configured `secrets.VSCODE_MCP_DISPATCH_TOKEN`, eliminating the previous error related to missing parameters.

items.title: Enhance OAuth 2.1 Support for Claude.ai Connectors in M C P Veo 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces OAuth 2.1 support for Claude.ai connectors, enhancing user authentication through AceDataCloud's AuthBackend. Key modifications include the addition of a new OAuth provider and updates to the API cost model to accurately account for cached tokens, preventing overcharging. All existing tests have been updated and successfully pass, ensuring stability and reliability.

items.title: Fix signature issues in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a 400 error caused by invalid signatures in thinking blocks when using the Claude Messages API. By stripping thinking blocks from assistant messages in the conversation history, we ensure that requests can be routed to different upstream accounts without signature validation failures. Non-thinking content remains intact, preserving the integrity of the messages.

items.title: Fix overcharging by adjusting cached tokens in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses overcharging issues caused by the inclusion of cached tokens in OpenAI's prompt tokens. The consumption formulas for 19 models have been modified to apply discounts correctly, ensuring accurate billing. Additionally, new tests have been added to verify the correct application of these discounts, with all existing tests passing successfully.

items.title: Revamp GitHub SEO for AceDataCloud Profile in .github 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the organization profile README to improve GitHub discovery. Key changes include highlighting flagship products, restructuring content for better readability, and strengthening messaging around global developer positioning. The modifications aim to create a more engaging and informative profile for users.

items.title: Fix billing overcharges for cached tokens in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses billing discrepancies for OpenAI models by incorporating discounts for cached tokens in the consumption formulas. Key changes include modifications to the `_chat_models.json` file to apply negative offsets for cached tokens, ensuring accurate billing. Additionally, new tests have been added to verify the correct application of these discounts, with all tests passing successfully.
